slug: 难民是怎么变成_问题_的
title: 难民是怎么变成"问题"的

outline.concept: Problematization is a political act, not a description. The shift from 'refugee crisis' (something happening to people) to 'refugee problem' (people who are the problem) redirects responsibility — away from the war or disaster that displaced them, onto the displaced themselves. Naming is the first move of control.
outline.query: 难民问题 危机 媒体 报道 标题

outline.concept: Statistical dehumanization: when individual stories become aggregate numbers, the brain's threat-detection activates but empathy shuts down. 'One family fleeing bombs' triggers compassion; '10,000 arrivals this month' triggers alarm. The switch from narrative to count is the switch from human to problem.
outline.query: 难民 数量 统计 涌入 新闻 大批

outline.concept: The refugee 'problem' is politically useful because a visible external threat justifies emergency powers, consolidates in-group identity, and distracts from domestic policy failures. Politicians don't create refugees, but they create the problem — because the problem does political work.
outline.query: 选举 移民 边境 威胁 政治辩论 外部敌人

outline.concept: Self-fulfilling framing: once treated as a problem, the policy response — camps, work bans, segregation, bureaucratic limbo — produces the very dysfunctions (poverty, parallel societies, desperation) that retroactively justify the 'problem' label. The frame manufactures the reality it claims to describe.
outline.query: 难民营 隔离 限制 不能工作 边缘化 政策
